samples.gvd:
module asyn_fifo is an asynchronous FIFO (First-In-First-Out) buffer with configurable width and depth. It has two separate clock domains for read and write operations, making it asynchronous. The FIFO uses Gray code for its read and write pointers, which are synchronized to the respective clock domains.

The module consists of the following parts:

1. Dual-Port RAM: This is the data storage unit of the FIFO. It has two separate ports, one for read operations and one for write operations. The write and read operations are performed independently, allowing for asynchronous operation.

2. Write Controller: This module controls the write operations. It manages the write pointer and determines when the FIFO is full.

3. Read Controller: This module controls the read operations. It manages the read pointer and determines when the FIFO is empty.

4. Read Pointer Synchronizer: This module synchronizes the read pointer from the write clock domain to the read clock domain.

5. Write Pointer Synchronizer: This module synchronizes the write pointer from the read clock domain to the write clock domain.

The empty and full signals are generated by comparing the Gray-coded read and write pointers. When the FIFO is empty, the read pointer is equal to the write pointer. When the FIFO is full, the most significant two bits of the write pointer are different from the most significant two bits of the read pointer, and the remaining bits are the same.

The module uses the following parameters:
- WIDTH: The width of the data bus, which is configurable.
- DEPTH: The depth of the FIFO, which is also configurable.

The module uses the following input ports:
- wclk: The write clock signal.
- rclk: The read clock signal.
- wrstn: The write reset signal, active low.
- rrstn: The read reset signal, active low.
- winc: The write increment signal.
- rinc: The read increment signal.
- wdata: The write data input.

The module uses the following output ports:
- wfull: The write full signal.
- rempty: The read empty signal.
- rdata: The read data output.

The module uses the following internal registers:
- write_pointer: The write pointer, using Gray code.
- read_pointer: The read pointer, using Gray code.
- read_pointer_g: The read pointer synchronized to the write clock domain, using Gray code.
- write_pointer_g: The write pointer synchronized to the read clock domain, using Gray code.

The module uses the following internal wires:
- write_enable: The write enable signal, generated by the write controller.
- read_enable: The read enable signal, generated by the read controller.

The module uses the following submodules:
- dual_port_ram: The dual-port RAM for storing the data.

The module uses the following synchronization logic:
- The read pointer is synchronized to the write clock domain using a two-stage trigger of the write clock.
- The write pointer is synchronized to the read clock domain using a two-stage trigger of the read clock.

The module uses the following empty and full logic:
- The FIFO is considered empty when the read pointer is equal to the write pointer.
- The FIFO is considered full when the most significant two bits of the write pointer are different from the most significant two bits of the read pointer, and the remaining bits are the same.

The module uses the following behavioral code:
- The write controller generates the write enable signal and updates the write pointer.
- The read controller generates the read enable signal and updates the read pointer.
- The read pointer synchronizer synchronizes the read pointer to the write clock domain.
- The write pointer synchronizer synchronizes the write pointer to the read clock domain.
- The read data output is updated when the read enable signal is active.

This asynchronous FIFO design allows for independent read and write operations, with the read and write pointers synchronized to their respective clock domains. The FIFO is considered full when the write pointer is one cycle ahead of the read pointer in the Gray code representation, and it is considered empty when the read and write pointers are equal.

The module can be instantiated with the desired width and depth, and it can be used in various digital systems that require an asynchronous FIFO buffer.
module asyn_fifo #(
    parameter WIDTH = 8,
    parameter DEPTH = 16
)(
    input wclk,
    input rclk,
    input wrstn,
    input rrstn,
    input winc,
    input rinc,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata,
    output wfull,
    output rempty,
    output [WIDTH-1:0] rdata
);

localparam ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH);

reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] write_pointer, read_pointer;
reg [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] read_pointer_g, write_pointer_g;

wire write_enable, read_enable;

dual_port_ram #(
    .WIDTH(WIDTH),
    .DEPTH(DEPTH)
) dual_port_ram_inst (
    .wclk(wclk),
    .wenc(write_enable),
    .waddr(write_pointer[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]),
    .wdata(wdata),
    .rclk(rclk),
    .renc(read_enable),
    .raddr(read_pointer[ADDR_WIDTH-2:0]),
    .rdata(rdata)
);

// Write controller
always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
    if (!wrstn) begin
        write_pointer <= 0;
    end else if (winc && !wfull) begin
        write_pointer <= write_pointer + 1;
    end
end

// Read controller
always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
    if (!rrstn) begin
        read_pointer <= 0;
    end else if (rinc && !rempty) begin
        read_pointer <= read_pointer + 1;
    end
end

// Read pointer synchronizer
always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
    if (!rrstn) begin
        read_pointer_g <= 0;
    end else begin
        read_pointer_g <= write_pointer;
    end
end

// Write pointer synchronizer
always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
    if (!wrstn) begin
        write_pointer_g <= 0;
    end else begin
        write_pointer_g <= read_pointer;
    end
end

// Empty and full signals
assign rempty = (read_pointer == write_pointer);
assign wfull = (read_pointer_g[ADDR_WIDTH-1:ADDR_WIDTH-2] != write_pointer[ADDR_WIDTH-1:ADDR_WIDTH-2]) && (read_pointer_g[ADDR_WIDTH-3:0] == write_pointer[ADDR_WIDTH-3:0]);

// Write enable and read enable signals
assign write_enable = winc && !wfull;
assign read_enable = rinc && !rempty;

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">Data width of FIFO.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">Depth of FIFO.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <para>1. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule.</para>
    <para>2. Use the first 4 bits of the read/write pointer as the address of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>3. Use the 5th bit of the read/write pointer as the read/write enable of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>4. Use the 5th bit of the synchronized read pointer as the write increment input.</para>
    <para>5. Use the 5th bit of the synchronized write pointer as the read increment input.</para>
    <para>6. Use the 5th bit of the write pointer as the write enable of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>7. Use the 5th bit of the synchronized read pointer as the read enable of the dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>8. Use the 5th bit of the write pointer as the write increment input.</para>
    <para>9. Use the 5th bit of the synchronized read pointer as the read increment input.</para>
    <para>10. Use the 5th bit of the write pointer as the write increment input.</para>
    <para>11. Use the 5th bit of the synchronized read pointer as the read increment input.</para>
